column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en

BG 17.1#अर्जुन उवाच
ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विता:
तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तम:#arjuna uvācha
ye śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥ
teṣhāṁ niṣhṭhā tu kā kṛiṣhṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ#Arjun said: O Krishna, where do they stand who disregard the injunctions of the scriptures, but still worship with faith? Is their faith in the mode of goodness, passion, or ignorance?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 17.2#श्रीभगवानुवाच
त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां शृणु#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
tri-vidhā bhavati śhraddhā dehināṁ sā svabhāva-jā
sāttvikī rājasī chaiva tāmasī cheti tāṁ śhṛiṇu#The Supreme Divine Personality said: Every human being is born with innate faith, which can be of three kinds—sāttvic, rājasic, or tāmasic. Now hear about this from Me.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Personality said

BG 17.3#सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत\nश्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्ध: स एव स:#sattvānurūpā sarvasya śhraddhā bhavati bhārata\nśhraddhā-mayo ‘yaṁ puruṣho yo yach-chhraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ#The faith of all humans conforms to the nature of their mind. All people possess faith, and whatever the nature of their faith, that is verily what they are.#sattva-anurūpā—conforming to the nature of one’s mind
column10: yat-śhraddhaḥ—whatever the nature of their faith

column1: BG 17.4#यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसा:\nप्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जना:#yajante sāttvikā devān yakṣha-rakṣhānsi rājasāḥ\npretān bhūta-gaṇānśh chānye yajante tāmasā janāḥ#Those in the mode of goodness worship the celestial gods
column2: those in the mode of passion worship the yakṣhas and rākṣhasas
column3: those in the mode of ignorance worship ghosts and spirits.#yajante—worship
column4: sāttvikāḥ—those in the mode of goodness
column6: yakṣha—semi-celestial beings who exude power and wealth
column7: rakṣhānsi—powerful beings who embody sensual enjoyment, revenge, and wrath
column8: rājasāḥ—those in the mode of passion
column13: tāmasāḥ—those in the mode of ignorance

column1:
BG 17.5#अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जना:
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ता: कामरागबलान्विता:
कर्षयन्त: शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतस:
मां चैवान्त:शरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान्#aśhāstra-vihitaṁ ghoraṁ tapyante ye tapo janāḥ
dambhāhankāra-sanyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥ
karṣhayantaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ bhūta-grāmam achetasaḥ
māṁ chaivāntaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ tān viddhy āsura-niśhchayān#BG 17.5-6:#aśhāstra-vihitam—not enjoined by the scriptures

BG 17.6#अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जना:
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ता: कामरागबलान्विता:
कर्षयन्त: शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतस:
मां चैवान्त:शरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान्#aśhāstra-vihitaṁ ghoraṁ tapyante ye tapo janāḥ
dambhāhankāra-sanyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥ
karṣhayantaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ bhūta-grāmam achetasaḥ
māṁ chaivāntaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ tān viddhy āsura-niśhchayān#BG 17.5-6:#aśhāstra-vihitam—not enjoined by the scriptures

BG 17.7#आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रिय:\nयज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं शृणु#āhāras tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyaḥ\nyajñas tapas tathā dānaṁ teṣhāṁ bhedam imaṁ śhṛiṇu#The food that people prefer is according to their dispositions. The same is true for the sacrifice, austerity, and charity they are inclined (or predisposed) toward. Now hear of the distinctions from Me.#āhāraḥ—food

BG 17.8#आयु:सत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धना:\nरस्या: स्निग्धा: स्थिरा हृद्या आहारा: सात्त्विकप्रिया:#āyuḥ-sattva-balārogya-sukha-prīti-vivardhanāḥ\nrasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛidyā āhārāḥ sāttvika-priyāḥ#Persons in the mode of goodness prefer foods that promote life span, and increase virtue, strength, health, happiness, and satisfaction. Such foods are juicy, succulent, nourishing, and naturally tasteful.#āyuḥ sattva—which promote longevity
column12: sāttvika-priyāḥ—dear to those in the mode of goodness

column1: BG 17.9#कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिन:\nआहारा राजसस्येष्टा दु:खशोकामयप्रदा:#kaṭv-amla-lavaṇāty-uṣhṇa- tīkṣhṇa-rūkṣha-vidāhinaḥ\nāhārā rājasasyeṣhṭā duḥkha-śhokāmaya-pradāḥ#Foods that are too bitter, too sour, salty, very hot, pungent, dry, and full of chillies, are dear to persons in the mode of passion. Such foods produce pain, grief, and disease.#kaṭu—bitter
column9: rājasasya—to persons in the mode of passion

column1: BG 17.10#यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत्\nउच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम्#yāta-yāmaṁ gata-rasaṁ pūti paryuṣhitaṁ cha yat\nuchchhiṣhṭam api chāmedhyaṁ bhojanaṁ tāmasa-priyam#Foods that are overcooked, stale, putrid, polluted, and impure are dear to persons in the mode of ignorance.#yāta-yāmam—stale foods
column12: tāmasa—to persons in the mode of ignorance

column1: BG 17.11#अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते\nयष्टव्यमेवेति मन: समाधाय स सात्त्विक:#aphalākāṅkṣhibhir yajño vidhi-driṣhṭo ya ijyate\nyaṣhṭavyam eveti manaḥ samādhāya sa sāttvikaḥ#Sacrifice that is performed according to scriptural injunctions without expectation of rewards, with the firm conviction of the mind that it is a matter of duty, is of the nature of goodness.#aphala-ākāṅkṣhibhiḥ—without expectation of any reward
column3: vidhi-driṣhṭaḥ—that is in accordance with the scriptural injunctions

column1: BG 17.12#अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत्\nइज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम्#abhisandhāya tu phalaṁ dambhārtham api chaiva yat\nijyate bharata-śhreṣhṭha taṁ yajñaṁ viddhi rājasam#O best of the Bharatas, know that sacrifice performed for material benefit, or with a hypocritical aim, is in the mode of passion.#abhisandhāya—motivated by
column11: bharata-śhreṣhṭha—Arjun, the best of the Bharatas

column1: BG 17.13#विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम्\nश्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते#vidhi-hīnam asṛiṣhṭānnaṁ mantra-hīnam adakṣhiṇam\nśhraddhā-virahitaṁ yajñaṁ tāmasaṁ parichakṣhate#Sacrifice devoid of faith and contrary to the injunctions of the scriptures, in which no food is offered, no mantras chanted, and no donation made, is to be considered in the mode of ignorance.#vidhi-hīnam—without scriptural direction
column3: mantra-hīnam—with no chanting of the Vedic hymns
column4: adakṣhiṇam—with no remunerations to the priests

column1: BG 17.14#देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम्\nब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते#deva-dwija-guru-prājña- pūjanaṁ śhaucham ārjavam\nbrahmacharyam ahinsā cha śhārīraṁ tapa uchyate#When worship of the Supreme Lord, the Brahmins, the spiritual master, the wise, and the elders is done with the observance of cleanliness, simplicity, celibacy, and non-violence then this worship is declared as the austerity of the body.#deva—the Supreme Lord

BG 17.15#अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्\nस्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते#anudvega-karaṁ vākyaṁ satyaṁ priya-hitaṁ cha yat\nsvādhyāyābhyasanaṁ chaiva vāṅ-mayaṁ tapa uchyate#Words that do not cause distress, are truthful, inoffensive, and beneficial, as well as regular recitation of the Vedic scriptures—these are declared as austerity of speech.#anudvega-karam—not causing distress

BG 17.16#मन: प्रसाद: सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रह:\nभावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते#manaḥ-prasādaḥ saumyatvaṁ maunam ātma-vinigrahaḥ\nbhāva-sanśhuddhir ity etat tapo mānasam uchyate#Serenity of thought, gentleness, silence, self-control, and purity of purpose—all these are declared as austerity of the mind.#manaḥ-prasādaḥ—serenity of thought

BG 17.17#श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरै:\nअफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तै: सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते#śhraddhayā parayā taptaṁ tapas tat tri-vidhaṁ naraiḥ\naphalākāṅkṣhibhir yuktaiḥ sāttvikaṁ parichakṣhate#When devout persons with ardent faith practice these three-fold austerities without yearning for material rewards, they are designated as austerities in the mode of goodness.#śhraddhayā—with faith

BG 17.18#सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत्\nक्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम्#satkāra-māna-pūjārthaṁ tapo dambhena chaiva yat\nkriyate tad iha proktaṁ rājasaṁ chalam adhruvam#Austerity that is performed with ostentation for the sake of gaining honor, respect, and adoration is in the mode of passion. Its benefits are unstable and transitory.#sat-kāra—respect

BG 17.19#मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तप:\nपरस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्#mūḍha-grāheṇātmano yat pīḍayā kriyate tapaḥ\nparasyotsādanārthaṁ vā tat tāmasam udāhṛitam#Austerity that is performed by those with confused notions, and which involves torturing the self or harming others, is described to be in the mode of ignorance.#mūḍha—those with confused notions

BG 17.20#दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे\nदेशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम्#dātavyam iti yad dānaṁ dīyate ‘nupakāriṇe\ndeśhe kāle cha pātre cha tad dānaṁ sāttvikaṁ smṛitam#Charity given to a worthy person simply because it is right to give, without consideration of anything in return, at the proper time and in the proper place, is stated to be in the mode of goodness.#dātavyam—worthy of charity
column6: anupakāriṇe—to one who cannot give in return

column1: BG 17.21#यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुन:\nदीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम्#yat tu pratyupakārārthaṁ phalam uddiśhya vā punaḥ\ndīyate cha parikliṣhṭaṁ tad dānaṁ rājasaṁ smṛitam#But charity given with reluctance, with the hope of a return or in expectation of a reward, is said to be in the mode of passion.#yat—which
column3: prati-upakāra-artham—with the hope of a return

column1: BG 17.22#अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते\nअसत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्#adeśha-kāle yad dānam apātrebhyaśh cha dīyate\nasat-kṛitam avajñātaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛitam#And that charity, which is given at the wrong place and wrong time to unworthy persons, without showing respect, or with contempt, is held to be of the nature of nescience.#adeśha—at the wrong place

BG 17.23#ॐ तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविध: स्मृत:\nब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिता: पुरा#oṁ tat sad iti nirdeśho brahmaṇas tri-vidhaḥ smṛitaḥ\nbrāhmaṇās tena vedāśh cha yajñāśh cha vihitāḥ purā#The words “Om Tat Sat” have been declared as symbolic representations of the Supreme Absolute Truth, from the beginning of creation. From them came the priests, scriptures, and sacrifice.#om tat sat—syllables representing aspects of transcendence

BG 17.24#तस्माद् ॐ इत्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतप:क्रिया:\nप्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ता: सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम्#tasmād oṁ ity udāhṛitya yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ\npravartante vidhānoktāḥ satataṁ brahma-vādinām#Therefore, when performing acts of sacrifice, offering charity, or undertaking penance, expounders of the Vedas always begin by uttering “Om” according to the prescriptions of Vedic injunctions.#tasmāt—therefore
column10: vidhāna-uktāḥ—according to the prescriptions of Vedic injunctions

column1: BG 17.25#तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतप:क्रिया:\nदानक्रियाश्च विविधा: क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभि:#tad ity anabhisandhāya phalaṁ yajña-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ\ndāna-kriyāśh cha vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣha-kāṅkṣhibhiḥ#Persons who do not desire fruitive rewards, but seek to be free from material entanglements, utter the word “Tat” along with acts of austerity, sacrifice, and charity.#tat—the syllable Tat
column13: mokṣha-kāṅkṣhibhiḥ—by seekers of freedom from material entanglements

column1:
BG 17.26#सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्द: पार्थ युज्यते
यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थिति: सदिति चोच्यते
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते#sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyate
praśhaste karmaṇi tathā sach-chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyate
yajñe tapasi dāne cha sthitiḥ sad iti chochyate
karma chaiva tad-arthīyaṁ sad ity evābhidhīyate#BG 17.26-27:#sat-bhāve—with the intention of eternal existence and goodness

BG 17.27#सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्द: पार्थ युज्यते
यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थिति: सदिति चोच्यते
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते#sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyate
praśhaste karmaṇi tathā sach-chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyate
yajñe tapasi dāne cha sthitiḥ sad iti chochyate
karma chaiva tad-arthīyaṁ sad ity evābhidhīyate#BG 17.26-27:#sat-bhāve—with the intention of eternal existence and goodness

BG 17.28#अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत्\nअसदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह#aśhraddhayā hutaṁ dattaṁ tapas taptaṁ kṛitaṁ cha yat\nasad ity uchyate pārtha na cha tat pretya no iha#O son of Pritha, whatever acts of sacrifice, charity, or penance are done without faith, are termed as “Asat.” They are useless both in this world and the next.#aśhraddhayā—without faith
